evals.prompt: steelman and red team this for me: raising the minimum wage to $25/hour in the US would be net positive for low-income workers
evals.expected_output: Four-section analysis: steelman of the claim (best case for $25 minimum wage helping low-income workers), red-team of that steelman (strongest attacks on it), steelman of the counter-claim (best case that it would hurt low-income workers), red-team of the counter-claim's steelman, then a synthesis identifying the strongest signals and giving a verdict or naming the key unresolved crux.

assertions.name: has-four-sections
assertions.description: Output contains all four argument sections plus synthesis

assertions.name: steelmans-cite-evidence
assertions.description: The steelmans reference specific evidence, studies, or reasoning (not just vague assertions)
assertions.criterion: Both steelman sections contain specific empirical evidence, economic reasoning, or concrete examples — not just vague assertions

assertions.name: red-teams-find-real-weaknesses
assertions.description: Red-teams identify genuine vulnerabilities, not strawmen
assertions.criterion: Both red-team sections attack the *steelmanned* version of each argument (not a weaker version), and identify vulnerabilities that a thoughtful proponent would find genuinely challenging

assertions.name: synthesis-gives-verdict
assertions.description: Synthesis arrives at a conclusion or clearly names the crux
assertions.criterion: The synthesis section provides a specific conclusion, verdict, or clearly names the unresolved empirical question or value disagreement at the heart of the debate — not just 'it depends' without explanation

assertions.name: synthesis-explains-reasoning-chain
assertions.description: Synthesis traces how the four arguments led to the conclusion
assertions.criterion: The synthesis explains how the four preceding arguments (what survived, what was damaged, what converged or contradicted) led to its conclusion

evals.prompt: Can you steelman and red team the idea that social media has made teenage mental health significantly worse?
evals.expected_output: Four-section analysis covering the claim (social media worsening teen mental health), its red-team, the counter-claim (social media hasn't significantly worsened teen mental health, or other factors explain it), its red-team, and synthesis.

assertions.name: has-four-sections
assertions.description: Output contains all four argument sections plus synthesis
assertions.criterion: The response has clearly delineated steelman of the claim, red-team of that steelman, steelman of the counter-claim, red-team of the counter-claim, and synthesis

assertions.name: counter-claim-is-positive-case
assertions.description: Counter-claim steelman is a positive argument, not just negation
assertions.criterion: The counter-claim steelman makes a positive, affirmative case for why social media hasn't significantly worsened teen mental health (or why other factors are more responsible) — it's not just 'the original claim is wrong' but a substantive alternative view

assertions.name: references-research
assertions.description: Analysis engages with actual research on the topic
assertions.criterion: The analysis references specific researchers, studies, or bodies of evidence relevant to teen mental health and social media (e.g., Haidt, Twenge, or critics like Orben/Przybylski)

assertions.name: synthesis-handles-contradiction
assertions.description: If signals are contradictory, synthesis says so explicitly
assertions.criterion: If the four arguments point in genuinely contradictory directions, the synthesis explicitly acknowledges the contradiction rather than papering over it

evals.prompt: I want you to steelman and red team this: companies should eliminate annual performance reviews and replace them with continuous feedback only
evals.expected_output: Four-section analysis of the HR/management proposal: steelman (best case for eliminating annual reviews), red-team of that, steelman of keeping annual reviews (or the counter-position), red-team of that, synthesis with verdict.

assertions.name: has-four-sections
assertions.description: Output contains all four argument sections plus synthesis
assertions.criterion: The response has clearly structured steelman of the claim, red-team of that steelman, steelman of the counter-claim, red-team of the counter-claim, and synthesis — all five sections present

assertions.name: steelmans-are-genuinely-strong
assertions.description: Each steelman is the strongest reasonable version of its position
assertions.criterion: Both steelman sections represent the strongest reasonable case for their position — someone who holds that view would recognize it as a fair and compelling presentation, not a weakened version

assertions.name: synthesis-gives-actionable-conclusion
assertions.description: Synthesis gives a conclusion useful to a manager or executive making this decision
assertions.criterion: The synthesis gives a conclusion that would be genuinely useful to a business leader deciding on performance review policy — either a clear recommendation, a clear identification of when each approach is better, or a precise naming of the values/empirical question that drives the disagreement
